email-service.config.connect.title: Connect your Worker to Email Service
email-service.config.connect.description: Connect to Email Service from your Worker using the send_email binding. Configure the binding in your Wrangler configuration file, then use the email handler in your Worker code to process and send emails programmatically.

email-service.common.suppressions.title: Suppressions
email-service.emailSending.title: Email Sending
email-service.emailSending.description: Send emails to your users from your domain reliably, securely, and at scale.
email-service.emailSending.connect.pageS: Process and send emails programmatically from Workers, REST clients, or SMTP libraries.
email-service.emailSending.connect.intro: Use the Email Sending Worker binding to dispatch transactional emails directly from your Worker code. No API token required.
Call the Cloudflare REST API or one of the official SDKs from any backend. Authenticate with a Cloudflare API token scoped to the Email Sending: Edit permission.
Authenticate to Cloudflare's SMTP relay with your API token to send messages from libraries and mail clients that already speak SMTP.
email-service.emailSending.connect.metho: Develop and deploy with wrangler: `npm create cloudflare@latest` · `npx wrangler dev` · `npm run deploy`
email-service.emailSending.connect.worke: Configure the binding in your Wrangler config, then call `env.EMAIL.send()` from your Worker.
email-service.emailSending.connect.restA: Create an API token with the `Email Sending: Edit` permission to authenticate your requests.
Create an Email Sending API token
This token allows the Email Sending API to send messages on behalf of this account. See the [docs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/email-service/api/send-emails/rest-api/) for details.
Pick a language and copy the snippet. The token you just created is already inlined; otherwise the example reads it from the `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` environment variable.
Create an API token with the `Email Sending: Edit` permission. The token is used as the SMTP password.
Create an Email Sending API token
This token is used as the SMTP password when authenticating to Cloudflare's SMTP relay. See the [docs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/email-service/api/send-emails/smtp/) for details.
Pick a language and copy the snippet. The token you just created is already inlined; otherwise the example reads it from the `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` environment variable.

email-service.emailSending.subdomain.sup: Suppressed addresses will not receive emails sent from any subdomain in this zone.
email-service.emailSending.account.suppr: Suppressed addresses will not receive emails sent from any zone in this account.
email-service.emailSending.addSuppressio: Add an email address to the suppression list to prevent it from receiving your emails.
email-service.reputation.statusHealthyDe: No issues detected. Your sending reputation is in good standing.
email-service.reputation.statusWarningDe: Deliverability concerns detected. Review the metrics below.
email-service.reputation.statusAtRiskDes: Sending at risk of being paused. Immediate action required.
email-service.reputation.statusPausedDes: Sending paused. Follow the steps below to resume.
email-service.reputation.bounceRateDescr: Percentage of sent emails that bounced (hard + soft)
email-service.reputation.complaintRateDe: Percentage of delivered emails generating a spam complaint
email-service.reputation.deliveryRateDes: Percentage of sent emails successfully delivered
email-service.reputation.remediationBoun: Your bounce rate is elevated. Clean your recipient lists and remove invalid addresses. Consider implementing email verification at signup.
Your bounce rate is critically high. Your sending may be paused if this rate does not improve. Immediately audit recipient lists and halt sending to unverified addresses.
email-service.reputation.remediationComp: Your complaint rate is elevated. Ensure recipients have opted in and make unsubscribe links prominent.
Your complaint rate is critically high. Your sending may be paused if this rate does not improve. Review your sending practices immediately.
email-service.reputation.remediationSpam: Your content is triggering spam filters. Review email content for spam-like patterns.
Your content is being heavily flagged as spam. Your sending may be paused. Review and revise email templates immediately.
email-service.reputation.remediationDeli: Your delivery rate has dropped. Check bounce and complaint rates for underlying issues.
Your delivery rate is critically low. Your sending may be paused. Investigate delivery failures urgently.
email-service.reputation.quotaAccountWid: This quota is shared across all subdomains in your account
email-service.reputation.overQuotaDescri: This account has exceeded its daily sending quota.
email-service.emailRouting.title: Email Routing
email-service.emailRouting.description: Create custom email addresses and route email to your verified email addresses or Workers.
email-service.emailRouting.onboarding.su: Create custom email addresses to use whenever you do not want to share your primary email address.
email-service.emailRouting.onboarding.em: No available zones found for email routing
email-service.emailRouting.zoneList.empt: We don't store or look into your emails.
Anyone can create addresses for their domain.
Route to the mailbox you use the most, like Gmail or Outlook.
email-service.emailRouting.destinationAd: Manage addresses for use as the target of routing rules. Destination addresses are available on all zones on the account. You can create up to 200 destination addresses for your account.
Destination addresses are shared across every zone on this account. Manage the address list here, then use a verified address as the target of a routing rule for this zone.
Add destination addresses to route emails
No addresses match the current filter
Try clearing the filter or searching by a different term.
email-service.emailRouting.account.suppr: Emails from suppressed addresses will not be routed for any zone in this account.
email-service.emailRouting.zone.suppress: Emails from suppressed addresses will not be routed for this zone.
email-service.emailRouting.zone.rules.he: Create routing rules and set the action to take on received emails.
email-service.emailRouting.zone.rules.ac: Routes emails matching this rule to the destination address.
Routes emails matching this rule to a Worker.
Deletes emails matching this rule without routing them.
email-service.emailRouting.zone.rules.ed: Edit a routing rule and set the action to take on received emails.
email-service.emailRouting.zone.rules.cr: Create a routing rule and set the action to take on received emails.
email-service.emailRouting.zone.rules.ed: Edit the catch-all rule and set the action for all other email addresses in this zone.
email-service.emailRouting.zone.rules.wr: Updating this Wrangler-managed rule in the Dashboard will create drift from your local <0>wrangler.jsonc</0>. You can cancel and apply the changes below through Wrangler instead, or continue in the Dashboard and resolve the drift on your next deploy.

Wrangler does not support this change. Continuing in the Dashboard will create drift from your local <0>wrangler.jsonc</0>. You can cancel and apply the removal below through Wrangler instead, or continue and resolve the drift before your next deploy.

Deleting this Wrangler-managed rule in the Dashboard will create drift from your local <0>wrangler.jsonc</0>. You can cancel and apply the removal below through Wrangler instead, or continue and resolve the drift before your next deploy.

Updating this Wrangler-managed rule in the Dashboard will create drift from your local <0>wrangler.jsonc</0>. You can cancel and deploy the existing configuration through Wrangler instead, or continue in the Dashboard and resolve the drift on your next deploy.
email-service.emailRouting.zone.settings: Use the '+' separator to add detail to addresses matched by your routing rules (address+detail@example.com).
Subdomains are dependent on the domain. If the domain is disabled, subdomains will be disabled too.
Email Workers allow you to create custom scripts and process your emails according to your specific requirements. Need more advanced features? Go to Cloudflare Workers.
Create a worker to handle email routing rules.
Email Workers allow you to create custom scripts to handle your emails in different ways.
Once deployed, you'll be able to modify your code from the dashboard
Email Worker that allows you to add emails to your approved senders list and keep them out of the junk folder.
Email Worker that allows you to block spam. The email messages won't be delivered to your inbox.
Email Worker that sends you Slack notifications when specified emails are received.
Write a custom script to process emails according to your requirements.
Worker name must start with a letter or number and contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores.
